sections.fieldWeightsDescription: Controls how much each field type contributes to the overall confidence score. All weights should sum to approximately 100%.
sections.propertySettingsDescription: Penalties applied when property detection has issues - empty properties, too many options, or low match rates.
sections.contentPenaltiesDescription: Penalties for content quality issues like short/long content, generic phrases, and hedging language. All values are in percentages.
sections.confidenceExplanation:
How Confidence Scoring Works

The confidence score (0-100%) estimates how reliable an AI analysis result is. It combines two components:

1. Base Score (from AI)
Each field (title, description, keywords, properties) has an AI-reported confidence. These are combined using the weights below.

2. Validation Penalties
Heuristic checks detect potential quality issues and apply penalties:
• Length issues - Content too short or too long for SEO
• Generic content - Placeholder phrases or vague marketing language
• Hedging language - Words like "possibly" or "might" suggesting AI uncertainty
• Duplicate content - Same text across different languages
• Property issues - No properties detected, or too many options selected

Final Score = Base Score - Total Penalties (capped by Max Total Penalty)

Results below the Low Confidence Threshold are flagged for manual review.

helpText.metaTitleMaxLength: Maximum character count for SEO meta title
helpText.metaDescriptionMaxLength: Maximum character count for SEO meta description
helpText.keywordCount: Number of keywords to generate per product (3-5)
helpText.keywordsMaxCharacterLength: Maximum total character count for all keywords combined (max 255)
helpText.includeSeoAnalysis: Include SEO metadata (title, description, keywords) in analysis output
helpText.descriptionMaxLength: Maximum character count for customer-facing description
helpText.tone: Sets the tone of generated descriptions
helpText.includeProductDescription: Include product description in analysis output
helpText.languageSelectionInfo: English is required for AI analysis. Select additional languages to generate translated content.
helpText.scheduledTaskInterval: How often to check for unanalyzed products
helpText.apiKey: Your Google AI Studio API key for Gemini
helpText.enableApprovalWorkflow: Require manual approval before applying analysis results
helpText.eligibleProductTypes: Comma-separated list of product type names to include in AI analysis
helpText.apiBaseUrl: Base URL for the Gemini API (without version)
helpText.minutesSavedPerProperty: Estimated minutes saved when AI assigns product properties
helpText.minutesSavedPerSeo: Estimated minutes saved when AI generates SEO metadata
helpText.minutesSavedForBaseDescription: Estimated minutes saved for primary language description
helpText.minutesSavedPerAdditionalTranslation: Estimated minutes saved per additional translation
helpText.enableConfidenceThreshold: When approval workflow is disabled, use confidence scores to flag low-quality results for manual review
helpText.lowConfidenceThreshold: Results below this confidence score (%) will require manual review
helpText.genericPatterns: Regex patterns to detect generic/placeholder content (one pattern per line)
helpText.hedgingWords: Words indicating AI uncertainty (one word per line)
helpText.fieldWeightMetaTitle: Weight for meta title in confidence calculation. All weights should sum to 100%. Higher = more important. (0-100%)
helpText.fieldWeightMetaDescription: Weight for meta description in confidence calculation. All weights should sum to 100%. Higher = more important. (0-100%)
helpText.fieldWeightProductDescription: Weight for product description in confidence calculation. All weights should sum to 100%. Higher = more important. (0-100%)
helpText.fieldWeightSeoKeywords: Weight for SEO keywords in confidence calculation. All weights should sum to 100%. Higher = more important. (0-100%)
helpText.fieldWeightProperties: Weight for property detection in confidence calculation. All weights should sum to 100%. Higher = more important. (0-100%)
helpText.idealOptionsPerProperty: How many options per property is ideal (e.g., 1-3 for Style). If AI suggests more, it may indicate uncertainty. (1-10)
helpText.emptyPropertyPenalty: Score reduction when AI detects a property group but suggests no options for it. (0-100%)
helpText.excessOptionPenalty: Score reduction per option beyond the ideal count. Applied when AI suggests too many options (uncertainty signal). (0-100%)
helpText.noPropertiesPenalty: Score reduction when AI fails to detect any properties at all for the image. (0-100%)
helpText.lowPropertyMatchPenalty: Score reduction when less than 50% of AI-suggested options match your whitelisted property values. (0-100%)
helpText.minLengthRatio: Minimum content length as percentage of max. E.g., 33% of 60-char title = at least 20 chars required. (0-100%)
helpText.shortTitlePenalty: Score reduction when meta title is shorter than the minimum length ratio. (0-100%)
helpText.longTitlePenalty: Score reduction when meta title exceeds the configured max length. (0-100%)
helpText.shortDescriptionPenalty: Score reduction when product description is shorter than the minimum length ratio. (0-100%)
helpText.fewKeywordsPenalty: Score reduction when AI generates fewer SEO keywords than the configured minimum (60% of target). (0-100%)
helpText.genericContentPenalty: Score reduction when AI uses generic placeholder phrases like "high quality" or "perfect for any". (0-100%)
helpText.hedgingPenaltyPerInstance: Score reduction per uncertain word found (e.g., "possibly", "might", "appears to"). Signals AI uncertainty. (0-100%)
helpText.hedgingPenaltyMax: Maximum total penalty from hedging words, even if many are found. Caps the hedging penalty. (0-100%)
helpText.duplicateContentPenalty: Score reduction when the same text appears in multiple languages (indicates failed translation). (0-100%)
helpText.maxTotalPenalty: Maximum total penalty that can be applied. Prevents the score from dropping too low from combined penalties. (0-100%)

helpText.confidence:
Confidence is calculated using heuristics ("good enough" estimations). Warnings indicate areas worth reviewing - they don't always mean something is wrong.

Warning types:
• Content length: Too short/long for SEO
• Generic content: Placeholder or vague language
• Hedging language: AI uncertainty ("possibly", "might")
• Duplicate content: Same text across languages
• Property issues: No properties or too many options
• Response quality: Incomplete AI response

confidenceWarnings.title: Quality Warnings
confidenceWarnings.heuristicsExplainer: These warnings are based on heuristic checks - practical rules that flag potential issues. They may not always indicate real problems, but suggest areas worth reviewing.

contentLength.title: Content Length Issues
contentLength.description: Text fields are shorter or longer than configured limits. Short content may lack detail; overly long content may be truncated in search results.

genericContent.title: Generic Content Detected
genericContent.description: The AI may have used placeholder phrases or overly generic marketing language. Review for authenticity and product-specific details.

hedgingLanguage.title: Hedging Language
hedgingLanguage.description: Words like "possibly", "might", or "appears to" suggest the AI was uncertain. Consider reviewing these sections for accuracy.

duplicateContent.title: Duplicate Content
duplicateContent.description: Similar or identical content was detected across languages. This may indicate the AI failed to properly translate or localize the content.

propertyIssues.title: Property Detection Issues
propertyIssues.description: Problems with detected properties - either none were found, some have no options, or too many options suggest uncertainty in classification.

imageQuality.title: Image Quality
imageQuality.description: The source image resolution is low, which may affect the AI's ability to accurately analyze the product.

responseQuality.title: Response Quality
responseQuality.description: The AI's response was incomplete or showed low internal confidence. Results may need manual verification.
